data.title: Prime Drink Group Corp voluntarily applied for a MCTO and granted an extension to file Annual Financial Filings by Sept 29, 2025.
data.description: Montreal-based Prime Drink Group Corp. (CSE: PRME) has voluntarily requested and received a management cease trade order (MCTO) from the British Columbia Securities Commission (BCSC), their principal regulator. This order, issued on July 30, 2025, extends the deadline for filing their annual financial statements for the period ended March 31, 2025, along with related documents, to September 29, 2025. This follows previous announcements on July 30, 2025 and August 15, 2025. The MCTO was granted under National Policy 12-203 Management Cease Trade Orders (NP 12-203).

data.title: Federal appeals court rules most Trump tariffs are illegal, Trump warns of destruction, tariffs to remain for now.
data.description: On Friday night, a federal appeals court declared that the majority of President Donald Trump's tariffs are unlawful. Despite this ruling, the tariffs imposed by Trump will remain effective at the moment. Trump expressed concerns that this decision could potentially devastate the nation. The court's decision was detailed in a report titled "Most Trump Tariffs Ruled Illegal By Federal Appeals Court," available on Investor's Business Daily's platform.

data.title: Foresight Diagnostics and Roche settle dispute through licensing agreement for PhasED-Seq™ technology in Non-Hodgkin's Lymphoma.
data.description: Today, Foresight Diagnostics revealed an agreement with Roche Molecular Systems and Roche Sequencing Systems, marking the end of their legal dispute. The agreement involves a limited licensing deal and was announced on August 29, 2025 in Boulder, Colorado. This resolution will pave the way for cooperation between the parties, putting an end to the legal battle and facilitating collaboration in the future.

data.title: Premium Global Income Split Corp. reports $0.46M increase in net assets, with $21.79M total assets as of June 30, 2025, paying $0.38 Preferred and $0.48 Class A share distributions.
data.description: Premium Global Income Split Corp, listed as PGIC on TSX, disclosed its financial performance for the half-year ending June 30, 2025. The net assets for Class A shareholders saw a $0.46 million increase, equivalent to $0.25 per Class A share. As of June 30, 2025, the net assets attributable to Class A shareholders stood at $21.79 million, or $6.38 per Class A share. During this period, cash distributions were made at a rate of $0.38 per Preferred share and $0.48 per Class A share.

data.title: Eliza Labs is suing xAI over licensing fees, threatening legal action if demands aren't met. AI founder Shaw Walters announced.
data.description: Shaw Walters, the founder of Eliza Labs, stated that xAI is requiring higher licensing fees from the agentic AI platform. Failure to comply could result in legal consequences.
Dividends being paid to shareholders of BDRs.

data.title: Firstfund (TSXV: FFP) reports $64,991 net income for Q2 2025, with $70,087 unrealized gain on Vitality Products Inc.
data.description: VANCOUVER, BC, Aug. 29, 2025 - Consolidated Firstfund Capital Corp. (TSXV: FFP) has reported their financial results for the three months ending June 30, 2025. The Company recorded a net income of $64,991 or $0.01 per share, a significant turnaround from the net loss of $59,522 or $0.01 per share reported in the same period in 2024. Additionally, there was an unrealized net gain of $70,087 in the most recent quarter, compared to an unrealized net loss of $70,086 in 2024, attributed to the revaluation of the investments in Vitality Products Inc. (TSXV). Details of this story are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Elon Musk's attorney to lead $200M Dogecoin fund, sponsored by House of Doge, as meme coin treasuries rise.
data.description: Alex Spiro, the lawyer of Elon Musk, has been appointed as the chairman of a forthcoming Dogecoin treasury company with a $200 million backing from House of Doge. This move signifies the rise of memecoin treasury entities in the market.
Aja Health & Wellness Inc. to acquire Canadian Travel and Doctors Clinics assets. Share subscription deal with GEM Global Yield LLC. Delay in filing statements.

data.title: AutoShop Answers releases 2026 Key2Key Training Calendar after selling out all 2025 events. Premier provider for auto industry.
data.description: In Houston on August 29, 2025, AutoShop Answers unveiled its 2026 Key To Key To Callbacks Training Calendar. The company, known for offering top-notch business training for independent automotive service providers, reported a successful year in 2025 with all programs sold. This calendar will guide professionals in the industry through key training sessions in the upcoming year, emphasizing the importance of callbacks.

data.title: Mulvihill Canadian Bank Enhanced Yield ETF reports $9.54M increase in net assets, with $106.37M total assets. Paid $0.35 per Unit to unitholders.
data.description: The Mulvihill Canadian Bank Enhanced Yield ETF reported an increase in net assets of $9.54 million for the six months ending June 30, 2025, equating to $0.71 per unit. As of that date, net assets were $106.37 million or $8.98 per unit. Cash distributions of $0.35 per unit were paid out to unitholders during the period. The fund mainly invests in common shares of major Canadian banks - Bank of Montreal, The Bank of Nova Scotia, Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce, National Bank of Canada, Royal Bank of Canada, and The Toronto-Dominion Bank. It utilizes modest leverage of 25 percent to enhance dividend yields and option strategies to increase income and reduce volatility. The fund is managed by Mulvihill Capital Management Inc. and its units are listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the symbol CBNK.

data.title: Stock market fluctuates, Dow Jones down while Nasdaq slightly up. New Warren Buffett stock surges amid Trump teasing Putin move. Solar stocks stand strong.
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones experienced a decline while the Nasdaq saw a slight increase in its value. A new equity associated with Warren Buffett saw a rise in the stock market. President Donald Trump hinted at a potential action regarding Putin. Solar stocks performed well, demonstrating positive growth. The latest stock market news of the day includes the Dow and Nasdaq's stability, Trump's reference to a possible move involving Putin, and the continued rally of a new stock affiliated with Warren Buffett.

data.title: Voxtur delays Q2 2025 financial results filing, missed Aug 29 deadline. TSXV: VXTR; OTCQB: VXTRF.
data.description: Today, Voxtur Analytics Corp., a North American technology company focused on enhancing transparency in real estate lending, revealed that it will not meet the deadline to submit its financial results for the second quarter ending June 30, 2025. The company's stocks are listed as TSXV: VXTR and OTCQB: VXTRF. Based in Toronto and Tampa, the company aims to provide a more accessible ecosystem for real estate transactions. The missed deadline for reporting occurred on August 29, 2025.

data.title: AdventHealth Zephyrhills in Pasco County to build a new 35,000 sq ft medical office building to accommodate growth.
data.description: AdventHealth is set to continue expanding its presence in Pasco County by building a new medical office building at the AdventHealth Zephyrhills campus. The facility will offer approximately 35,000 square feet of extra space for both new and existing staff.

data.title: Avenue Living has $8.5 billion AUM, $1.2 billion acquisitions, and $600 million annual revenue. Expanding across 7 provinces & 20 states.
data.description: Avenue Living, a fast-growing real estate investment firm in North America, has reported a year of record growth with over 1,000 employees in seven provinces and 20 states. They have surpassed $8.5 billion in assets under management and completed over $1.2 billion in acquisitions fiscal year-to-date. The firm has nearly $600 million in annual recurring revenue. In May 2025, they received an investment-grade debt rating and issued $250 million in bonds. Mini Mall Storage Properties achieved a $500+ million acquisition facility, and Avenue Living launched a global expansion offering targeting $350 million. Their disciplined growth strategy and focus on resilient markets have driven strong performance and attractive returns throughout FY2025. Avenue Living manages workforce housing, self-storage, and farmland assets.
